meta.objective: Evaluate product catalog and marketplace/search APIs for trade intelligence platform feasibility

apis.rate_limits: Beta: limited availability, 10-min cooldown on error. Paid tier requires contacting ALIBABA_OPEN_API@service.alibaba.com
apis.data_returned: Product list (id, subject, category, group IDs, gmt_modified), product detail (prices, SKU prices, delivery terms, wholesale prices, owner member info, custom info including min_order_quantity). For sellers managing own product feeds.
apis.missing_data: No public search API for third-party catalog browsing. API is designed for sellers/ISVs to sync their own products with Alibaba. No supplier identity for cross-seller search, no ratings, no origin country via API.
apis.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: Alibaba.com has NO public product search/catalog API for third parties. The Open API at developer.alibaba.com is for sellers/ISVs to manage their OWN product data (ERP sync). It cannot be used to search or browse the Alibaba marketplace catalog. To get Alibaba product data, you must use scraping (Apify actors like 'alibaba-product-scraper' at ~$0.005/record) or third-party proxy APIs like TMAPI. The Alibaba.com Open API is effectively useless for TradeOS as a product data source.

apis.rate_limits: QPS ≤ 10 (China IP), QPS ≤ 5 (overseas IP). Daily base limit 5,000-10,000 calls. Enterprise tier can increase.
apis.data_returned: Product search by keyword/category, product detail (multi-language titles: EN/ZH, description, price tiers, currency, min_order_quantity, hasTradeAssurance, certificateTypes), category tree, top lists by sales/price/new. Supplier fields: business type, location, employee range, audit status, member type (Diamond/Gold/etc).
apis.missing_data: No FOB port data, no shipping cost estimates, no tariff/tax data, no payment terms, no delivery terms (Incoterms) via API. No direct API for supplier contact details (email/phone).
apis.notes: STRONGEST CANDIDATE for TradeOS. MIC is the ONLY major Chinese B2B platform with a genuine, documented public API for product search and detail retrieval by third parties. Enterprise registration required (1-3 day approval with business license). OAuth 2.0 + MD5 signing. Access token expires in 2h (recommend refresh every 1.5h). ~3.6M suppliers. However, no delivery/payment terms or shipping estimates — these would need to be modeled from user input. TP信用保障 (Trade Assurance) flag is available. Best fit for evidence intake (draft case creation) in TradeOS.

apis.rate_limits: Initial: 1 TPS / 8640 TPD. Scales with shipped revenue: 1 TPD per $0.05 revenue, up to 10 TPS max. Access revoked if no qualified sales in 30 days.
apis.available_regions: Global (all Amazon marketplaces: US, UK, DE, FR, JP, etc.)
apis.data_returned: Item search by keyword/category, product info (title, features, images, ASIN), offers (price, savings, merchant info, condition, delivery info — Prime/FBA eligibility, free shipping), reviews (count, average), browse nodes, variations/child ASINs.
apis.missing_data: No supplier identity (only merchant name for 3P sellers, but no business registration/origin country). No MOQ (B2C only). No origin country. No FOB/CIF pricing. No tariff data. No unit/pack size for wholesale. API license REQUIRES directing sales to Amazon — no independent commercial use allowed. Must be Amazon Associate.
apis.notes: B2C-only API, not suited for trade intelligence. License agreement requires all usage to direct sales to Amazon and earn Associate fees. Commercial/independent use is prohibited — TradeOS cannot repurpose this data for trade case creation. Amazon would block access. Useful ONLY for B2C price reference/comparison, but legally constrained. No wholesale data (MOQ, unit pricing, origin country).

apis.api_name: Google Merchant API (formerly Content API for Shopping)
apis.rate_limits: Based on Google Cloud project quotas. No published hard limits for standard reads. gRPC and REST supported. Can run multiple parallel requests.
apis.data_returned: Product data (title, description, price, availability, condition, GTIN, brand, shipping, images, link). Product status (destination approvals, data quality issues). ProductInput for writes.
apis.missing_data: This is a merchant self-service API — you can only read YOUR OWN products. It is NOT a product search or catalog API. No cross-merchant search. No supplier identity (you ARE the supplier). No MOQ, origin country, delivery terms, payment terms, reviews. Only returns data you uploaded.
apis.notes: NOT USEFUL for TradeOS. This API lets merchants manage their own Google Shopping product listings. It cannot be used to search, browse, or query other merchants' products. There is no public Google Shopping product search API for third parties. The Shopping Program (Buy on Google) and free listings APIs are all merchant-side only.

apis.rate_limits: Varies by API. Item feed: 10/hour (25MB). Pricing: 100/hour single item, 6/day bulk. Inventory: 50/hour. Sandbox: 30/hour per feed type.
apis.data_returned: Item setup/management, price management, inventory management, order management, promotions, returns, reports. For sellers managing their own listings.
apis.missing_data: This is a seller API — you can only manage YOUR OWN items. No product search/browse across all sellers. No supplier identity data. No origin country, delivery terms, payment terms, reviews of other suppliers. No MOQ (B2C retail). No catalog discovery API for third parties.
apis.notes: NOT USEFUL for TradeOS. Seller-side API only. No public product catalog/browse API. Good sandbox environment but requires Walmart seller account. Only relevant if TradeOS were to help users list products on Walmart. Rate limits are strict (6/day for bulk pricing).

apis.rate_limits: RapidAPI tier-dependent. Free tier typically limited (e.g., 100-500 calls/day). Paid tiers scale. Product Search API (Rakuten Advertising): 100 calls/min, max 5,000 results.
apis.data_returned: Product Search: product ID, name, brand name, URLs (PC/mobile/affiliate), images (small/medium), caption, release date, maker code/name, item count, sales item count, max/min/average price, review count and average, rank data, genre info. Item Ranking by genre/gender/age. Tag Search. Genre Search.
apis.missing_data: Japan-market focused. No supplier identity (Rakuten is retail marketplace, not B2B wholesale). No origin country, delivery terms, payment terms. No MOQ (B2C). No FOB/CIF pricing. No tariff/tax data. Product Search API (Rakuten Advertising) returns XML only.
apis.notes: LIMITED USEFULNESS. Two separate APIs: (1) Rakuten Marketplace via RapidAPI — Japan B2C product data with pricing, reviews, rankings. (2) Rakuten Advertising Product Search API — for affiliate/publisher use, XML-only. Neither is B2B wholesale. Could be used for price reference/intelligence on Japanese market products, but no trade-specific data (MOQ, origin, FOB). The RapidAPI platform adds a dependency layer. Best for 'enrichment only' use case.

apis.rate_limits: No official API — scraping only. Strong anti-bot (Imperva/Incapsula) requiring proxy rotation and warm-up.
apis.data_returned: Via scraping: product ID, name, URL, image, price range (FOB min/max), MOQ, lead time (days), FOB port, model number, supplier name, location, verification level (0-6), business type, certifications, years on platform, verified_manufacturer/supplier/assessed flags.
apis.missing_data: No official API at all. All data must be scraped (legal gray area, TOS violation risk). No payment terms. No shipping cost estimates. No tariff/tax. Delivery terms (Incoterms) not explicitly returned — FOB port is available. No review/rating scores per product.
apis.notes: NO OFFICIAL API. Data is accessible ONLY via scraping (Apify actors e.g., devcake/globalsources-products-scraper at ~$0.005/record). Strong anti-bot protection. The scraped data quality is excellent — includes FOB pricing, MOQ, lead times, verification levels — but reliability is dependent on scraping infrastructure staying functional. Legal risk from TOS violation. TradeOS should use this as a secondary/fallback data source, not primary.

apis.rate_limits: No official API — scraping only. No published rate limits.
apis.data_returned: Via scraping: company name, email, phone, address, product categories, certifications, locations, business type, years in business, supplier sub-categories. 70+ fields available via scrapers.
apis.missing_data: No official API. No pricing data on products (ThomasNet is a supplier directory, not a transactional marketplace). No MOQ directly. No FOB/CIF pricing. No shipping estimates. No reviews/ratings in the traditional sense. No delivery/payment terms.
apis.notes: NO PUBLIC API. Thomas Navigator is a PAID product data syndication platform ($425+/month for suppliers to manage their catalogs) — not a search API. Thomas Connect is for OEM-distributor data syndication. The ThomasNet website is a supplier directory with no product pricing. Scrapers exist on Apify (~$3.49/1000 suppliers) but only get supplier info, not trade-specific data. Limited value for TradeOS except as a supplier identity/location source for North American industrial sectors.

apis.rate_limits: Unknown — API is for receiving buyer inquiries, not catalog search. No published limits.
apis.data_returned: Official API: buyer inquiries (name, company, email, phone, message, product requirement) — only for sellers. Via scraping: product name, description, price, currency (INR/USD), min order qty, company name, city/state/country, business type (manufacturer/supplier/exporter/distributor/trader), certifications (ISO, GMP), established year, member since, buyer feedback count, response rate, super seller/premium seller/trust stamp badges, product specifications.
apis.missing_data: Official API is INQUIRY-ONLY (receiving leads), not catalog search. No product search API exists. No delivery terms, payment terms, FOB pricing, shipping estimates, tariff/tax data via official channels. Must scrape for product data.
apis.notes: OFFICIAL API IS FOR SELLERS ONLY — My Inquiry API lets sellers pull buyer inquiries into CRM. There is NO official product catalog search API. Product and supplier data is accessible only via scraping (Apify actors like 'tradeindia-product-scraper'). Good scraped data quality for Indian market supplier discovery. Relevant for TradeOS only if India-sourced trade cases are needed. No delivery/payment terms. Legal risk from scraping.

apis.rate_limits: Tier-dependent. Pay-as-you-go pricing. No published hard limits beyond API token quota.
apis.data_returned: Alibaba product search by keyword/category. Product details with price, MOQ, supplier name, supplier country, Gold Supplier status, Trade Assurance flag, verified supplier flag, response rate, delivery guarantee, price range filtering, sort by relevance/sales.
apis.missing_data: Third-party proxy API — not official. Reliability depends on TMAPI staying operational and within Alibaba's scraping tolerance. No delivery terms, payment terms, FOB pricing. No tariff/tax data. No shipping cost estimates. Data freshness depends on proxy's caching.
apis.notes: WORKAROUND for missing Alibaba public API. TMAPI is a third-party proxy that scrapes/scrapes Alibaba.com and exposes it as a structured API. It provides what Alibaba's official API does not: product search across the marketplace, supplier identity, MOQ, Trade Assurance flags. Risk: third-party dependency, potential for Alibaba blocking, data freshness unknown, TOS violation risk. Useful as interim solution but not production-grade for TradeOS.

top_recommendations: Made-in-China.com Open API — ONLY official B2B product search API with documented endpoints, commercial use allowed. Best fit for TradeOS draft case creation (score: 4/5).
Alibaba.com data via scraping actors (Apify) — best data quality for trade intelligence despite scraping risk (score: 3/5).
GlobalSources.com data via scraping — excellent trade-specific fields (FOB, MOQ, lead time, verification) but highest anti-bot risk (score: 3/5).
summary.critical_finding: Nearly all major B2B platforms (Alibaba, GlobalSources, ThomasNet, TradeIndia) do NOT offer public product search APIs for third-party use. Their APIs are seller-side only (managing own listings/receiving inquiries). Made-in-China.com is the sole exception with a genuine public search API. Any TradeOS integration with Alibaba/GlobalSources will require a scraping-based approach via Apify or similar infrastructure.
data_gaps_across_all_apis: Delivery terms / Incoterms (only FOB port available from some scraped sources)
Payment terms (none of the APIs provide this)
Tariff / tax / duty data (not provided by any marketplace API)
Origin country (available from MIC, Alibaba scrapers, GlobalSources scrapers)
Unit/pack size for wholesale (partial — MOQ available from most B2B sources)
next_steps: Evaluate Apify infrastructure for reliable scraping of Alibaba.com product data
Pursue Made-in-China.com enterprise API registration as primary structured data source
Assess legal/TOS risk of scraping GlobalSources.com vs. business value
Explore tariff data APIs (separate research track — e.g., USITC, UN Comtrade, Panjiva)
Consider hybrid approach: official APIs where available + scraped data + user-supplied evidence
